metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC3 (and the corresponding test) to cover at least one of `story`/`epic` in addition to `task`, or explicitly note that `task` is representative of all non-exempt types and no further AC is needed.
findings.finding: The Scope section describes the warning as applying to `task`/`story`/`epic` (all non-bug/session_log types), but AC3 only requires a test for `task` and the absence for `bug` — leaving `story` and `epic` untested and without a covering criterion.
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC3 to read: 'asserts the stderr warning for `task` (representative of task/story/epic) and its absence for `bug` and `session_log`.' Optionally add a parametrized test case for `story`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add AC test assertions (or extend the existing test) to verify the warning also fires for `story` and `epic` ticket types, matching the scope's stated coverage of all `ticket_type not in ("bug", "session_log")` cases.
findings.finding: AC only mandates a test asserting the warning fires for `task` and is absent for `bug`; `story` and `epic` — which the Scope section explicitly lists as warning targets — are not covered by any AC test assertion, leaving a gap where an implementation could skip warning for those types and still pass all stated tests.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — third bullet; Scope section ('task/story/epic')
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC bullet 3: 'A test in tests/integration/test_stdout_purity.py asserts the stderr warning for task, story, and epic and its absence for bug and session_log.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `epic` should be excluded from the warning (since epics are expected containers and P9 gates on leaf-ness via `ctx.children`, not ticket type), or explicitly document that the warning is intentionally imprecise at create time for epics.
findings.finding: The plan's exemption condition (`ticket_type not in ('bug', 'session_log')`) warns on `epic` creation, but P9's actual gate is `applicable = not ctx.children` (det_floor.py:645). Epics are expected containers; once they have children, P9 will not fire for them at review time. Warning at epic-create time is a false positive that does not mirror the real P9 trigger.
findings.suggested_fix: Limit the warning to `ticket_type in ('task', 'story')`, matching the ticket types that are most commonly leaves and will actually be flagged by P9. Or add a comment noting the intentional imprecision: at create time, epics have no children yet, so the warning fires, but it will not materialize as a P9 failure once children are added.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/__init__.py` (the `rebar.create()` library API, line ~155) as a caller of `create_core()` outside `_commands/`, and either extend the warning there or explicitly call it out as a deferred follow-up alongside the MCP note.
findings.finding: The library API caller `src/rebar/__init__.py` (line 155) invokes `create_core()` directly — outside `_commands/` — and will silently create non-bug/task/story/epic tickets without any warning. This consumer is not enumerated in the plan's Scope or listed as an acknowledged out-of-scope omission.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Scope/AC whether the `Warning:` line is emitted to stderr when `--output json` is active (e.g., 'warning is always emitted to stderr regardless of output format' or 'warning is suppressed in json mode and instead included as a `"warnings"` key in the JSON output').
findings.finding: The plan's decision rule for whether the Warning: line should be emitted (or suppressed) when `--output json` is active is never specified. The create_cli() function already has a json-output branch that prints a machine-readable JSON object to stdout; the plan says 'stdout stays pure' but does not state whether the stderr warning is also suppressed in json mode (to avoid breaking a machine consumer that parses stderr) or still emitted.
findings.location: Scope section / create_cli() --output json branch (composer.py lines 246-254)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extract the gate-exempt ticket-type set to a shared constant (e.g., `GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES` in a shared module) imported by both `orchestrator.py` and `composer.py`, so the two lists cannot silently diverge.
findings.finding: The exempt ticket-type set (`("bug", "session_log")`) is hardcoded in composer.py independently from the gate-exempt set in orchestrator.py:540, creating two separate places that must be kept in sync as ticket types evolve.
findings.suggested_fix: Define `GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES = frozenset({"bug", "session_log"})` in a shared location (e.g., `src/rebar/ticket_types.py` or the existing plan_review constants) and import it in both `orchestrator.py` and `composer.py`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Acknowledge in the plan (or in a code comment) that the existing `repo` fixture's `create task` call will start emitting a Warning to stderr after this change, and confirm this does not break existing test assertions in `test_stdout_purity.py`.
findings.finding: The existing `repo` fixture in `test_stdout_purity.py` (line 57) calls `_engine_run(r, 'create', 'task', 'purity probe ticket')`. After this change, that call will emit a Warning to stderr. The plan's AC adds a new test asserting the warning for `task`, but does not acknowledge that the existing fixture invocation will now also produce stderr output — which could affect the existing `test_list_full_stdout_is_pure_json` test's assumptions or require the fixture to be updated.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note in the Scope section: 'The existing `repo` fixture create-task call will emit the new warning to stderr; the existing test assertions do not inspect stderr so no fixture change is required.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the warning's exemption tuple `('bug', 'session_log')` is derived from / cross-referenced with the primary gate-exemption check in `workflow_ops.py:79` (not only `orchestrator.py:540`), and update the plan's 'Why' citation to point at the authoritative location.
findings.finding: The plan cites `orchestrator.py:540` as the location where 'bug/session_log are gate-exempt upstream', but reading that file confirms line 540 is inside `_progressive_refresh` (the drift re-review path), not the primary plan-review gate. The primary gate exemption lives in `workflow_ops.py:79-82`. The plan's conclusion ('bug/session_log are gate-exempt so the warning mirrors that exemption') is correct in effect, but the cited evidence file/line refers to a different code path. If the implementation team looks only at orchestrator.py:540 to define the exemption set, they may miss that workflow_ops.py independently governs the primary gate and could diverge (e.g., if a future type is added to one place but not the other).
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope / Why section, cite `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py:79` as the authoritative gate-exemption check, and note that `orchestrator.py:540` is the drift-refresh path's secondary exemption.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
